paper_title: Antifouling Paints and Invertebrate Assemblage Analysis

methods.method_name: Antifouling Paint Application and Structure Setup
methods.description: This method describes the selection and application of antifouling paints on wood boards, which are then installed on structures submerged in the Baía River to study their effectiveness in inhibiting the attachment of the golden mussel.

steps.description: Select five antifouling paints based on efficiency in inhibiting golden mussel attachment and low toxicity.
steps.searchable_summary: select antifouling paints based on efficiency and toxicity

steps.description: Build four structures using PVC tubes and wood boards for the artificial substrate.
steps.searchable_summary: construct structures with PVC tubes and wood boards

steps.description: Apply each of the five antifouling paints to a board, with one board having only primer and another remaining uncoated as control.
steps.searchable_summary: apply antifouling paints and primer to boards

steps.description: Submerge the structures in the Baía River.

methods.description: This method involves statistical analysis of the data collected from the submerged structures to evaluate the effectiveness of antifouling paints and the composition of invertebrate assemblages.

steps.description: Use R software with the vegan package to perform ANOVA on accumulated organic material and invertebrate factors.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA using R on organic material and invertebrate data

steps.description: Perform log transformations to achieve normality and homoscedasticity.
Use Spearman Correlation Coefficient to evaluate correlations between organic material and invertebrate densities.
Examine assemblage composition using NMDS and Bray-Curtis distance.
steps.searchable_summary: perform NMDS and calculate Bray-Curtis distance

steps.description: Test differences in community structure among paint types using ANOSIM.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct ANOSIM for community structure differences

steps.description: Calculate the coefficient of community loss using control and primer as references.
